{{ill|Yokoyama Faction|de=Yokoyama-Fraktion|german=Yokoyama-Fraktion|ja=横山党|lt=Yokoyama Clan|lt_ja=横山氏|lt_de=Yokoyama-Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Faction|1=Yokoyama Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542517}}
Others }} '''Ono-shi''' (小野氏) is a clan that takes "Ono" as its name, belonging to the {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Clan|1=Uji (clan)}} of {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clans|1=Clan}}. It was a clan active from the first half of the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th Century|1=7th century}} to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}. The {{ill|Surname|en|Surname|WD=Q101352|lt=Surname|1=Surname}} originally was that of a {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Retainer|1=Omi (title)}}, but was later elevated to {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}} by the {{ill|Eight-Colored Surname|de=Achtfarbeniger Familienname|german=Achtfarbeniger Familienname|ja=八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Colored Surname|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}. It is a branch of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Ware Clan|1=Wani clan}}, whose ancestor is {{ill|Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|de=Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|german=Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|ja=天足彦国押人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443593}}, a {{ill|Prince|en|Prince|WD=Q2747456|lt=Prince|1=Prince}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. There is also an Ono clan derived from place names, such as the one known as the Ono clan of the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}}, claiming descent from {{ill|Minamoto no Moroyoshi|de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|german=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|ja=源盛義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564234}}, who resided in Mino Province Ono. == 動向 == It was based around Ono Village in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District|1=Shiga District, Shiga}}, in what is now part of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Otsu City|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is also believed that Ono Township in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}{{ill|Atago District|de=Atago-Kreis|german=Atago-Kreis|ja=愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, in what is now within {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, was under their control. Moreover, within the {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} area of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, at the {{ill|Sūdō Shrine|de=Sūdō-Schrein|german=Sūdō-Schrein|ja=崇導神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 崇道神社|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}}, on Mt. Jōkō in the Nishimyōji area, is the tomb of {{ill|Ono no Kejin|de=Ono no Kejin|german=Ono no Kejin|ja=小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Kejin|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}}. The Ono clan was in charge of many maritime and military affairs, drawing on their skills in water transportation on Lake Biwa{{ill|Inoue Mitsurō|de=Inoue Mitsurō|german=Inoue Mitsurō|ja=井上満郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsurō|1=Mitsuo Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16264281}}’s “{{ill|Ono no Imoko: The Envoy to Sui I (Part 1)|de=Ono no Imoko: Botschafter an die Sui (Teil 1)|german=Ono no Imoko: Der Gesandte der Sui (Teil 1)|ja=遣隋使の小野妹子①|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Imoko: The Envoy to Sui I (Part 1)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}” (column "People Who Come Across" in {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|en|Kyoto Shimbun|WD=Q6452210|lt=Kyoto Shimbun|1=Kyoto Shimbun}}, October 17, 2021). Many members of the clan served as envoys, including {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}}, who became an envoy to the Sui, as well as envoys to the Tang. Numerous local bureaucrats in regions such as {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku|1=Tōhoku region}} and {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}} also hailed from this clan. While renowned for excellence in Chinese poetry and waka, with figures like {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}} who rose to the position of counselor, and {{ill|Ono no Michikaze|en|Ono no Michikaze|WD=Q1439775|lt=Ono no Michikaze|1=Ono no Michikaze}}, known as a calligrapher, the clan also produced figures such as {{ill|Ono no Harukaze|de=Ono Harukaze|german=Ono Harukaze|ja=小野春風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Harukaze|1=Harukaze Ono|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464105}}, who became a {{ill|Chinju Shōgun|de=Chinju Shōgun|german=Chinju Shōgun|ja=鎮守将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju Shōgun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守府将軍|1=Chinjufu shōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2700052}}, and {{ill|Ono no Yoshikoku|de=Ono no Yoshikoku|german=Ono no Yoshikoku|ja=小野好古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Yoshikoku|1=Ono no Yoshifuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463979}}, who, as an officer tasked with pursuing criminals, helped suppress the rebellion known as {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Fujiwara no Jun'yū Rebellion|1=Tengyō no Ran}}. The leading faction of the {{ill|Musashi Seven Factions|de=Musashi Sieben Parteien|german=Musashi Sieben Parteien|ja=武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Seven Factions|1=Q11546323|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}, the {{ill|Yokoyama Faction|de=Yokoyama-Fraktion|german=Yokoyama-Fraktion|ja=横山党|lt=Yokoyama Clan|lt_ja=横山氏|lt_de=Yokoyama-Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Faction|1=Yokoyama Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542517}}, is descended from {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}}. A branch of the Yokoyama (Inomata) clan, the {{ill|Yurayoshi|de=Yurayoshi|german=Yurayoshi|ja=由良氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yurayoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横瀬氏|1=Q11543327|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543327}} (also known as {{ill|Yokose|de=Yokose|german=Yokose|ja=横瀬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokose|1=Q11543327|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543327}}), also bears Ono as its clan name. == 清和源氏義光流平賀氏系小野氏 == The branch of the Ono clan of the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}} traces its ancestry to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}’s fourth son, {{ill|Minamoto no Moroyoshi|de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|german=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|ja=源盛義|lt=Minamoto no Moroyoshi (Hiraga Moroyoshi)|lt_ja=源盛義（平賀盛義）|lt_de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi (Hiraga Moroyoshi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564234}}, and is known as the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}}. During the era of {{ill|Tameyoshi|de=Tameyoshi|german=Tameyoshi|ja=大内惟義|lt=Tameyoshi|lt_ja=惟義|lt_de=Tameyoshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tameyoshi|1=Ōuchi Koreyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432524}}, the clan obtained the governorships of six provinces in the Kinki region—Ise, Iga, Echizen, Mino, Tanba, and Settsu—and held an important position within the Kamakura shogunate. It is said that Tameyoshi’s grandson, Yoshiyuki, resided in Ono of Mino Province and took the name Ono. One member of this clan, Ono Tsuguatsu, served under {{ill|Oe no Chikahiro|de=Oe no Chikahiro|german=Oe no Chikahiro|ja=大江親広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe no Chikahiro|1=Ōe no Chikahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436889}}, the legitimate son of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Oe no Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, but when Chikahiro, having been defeated in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}, went into hiding at {{ill|Sagae Manor|de=Sagae-Anwesen|german=Sagae-Anwesen|ja=寒河江荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae Manor|1=Q17224643|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17224643}} in {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|WD=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province|1=Dewa Province}}, he accompanied him. In the Jōkyū War, the head of the Hiraga clan, who inherited governorships over six provinces, namely {{ill|Tamenobu|de=Tamenobu|german=Tamenobu|ja=大内惟信|lt=Tamenobu|lt_ja=惟信|lt_de=Tamenobu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamenobu|1=Ōuchi Korenobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432522}}, also served under {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}, and was later exiled. Ono Tsuguatsu’s son, Akeno, is believed to have resided in the Kantō region, as Chikahiro’s son, Hirosaki, who was permitted after the rebellion, served the shogunate in Kamakura. However, around the late 13th century, when Oe no Motoken entered Sagae Manor, the Ono clan is thought to have also migrated south. Subsequently, they served as retainers of the Sagae clan, but after the Sagae clan was destroyed by the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|WD=Q524625|lt=Mogami Clan|1=Mogami clan}}, in the 12th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1584), they returned to farming. == 系図 == {{ill|Oblique type|en|Oblique type|WD=Q3232321|lt=Italicized|1=Oblique type}} indicates female. {{ill|Yokoyama Faction|de=Yokoyama-Fraktion|german=Yokoyama-Fraktion|ja=横山党|lt=Yokoyama Clan|lt_ja=横山氏|lt_de=Yokoyama-Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Faction|1=Yokoyama Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542517}}
Others }} '''Ono-shi''' (小野氏) is a clan that takes "Ono" as its name, belonging to the {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Clan|1=Uji (clan)}} of {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clans|1=Clan}}. It was a clan active from the first half of the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th Century|1=7th century}} to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}. The {{ill|Surname|en|Surname|WD=Q101352|lt=Surname|1=Surname}} originally was that of a {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Retainer|1=Omi (title)}}, but was later elevated to {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}} by the {{ill|Eight-Colored Surname|de=Achtfarbeniger Familienname|german=Achtfarbeniger Familienname|ja=八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Colored Surname|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}. It is a branch of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Ware Clan|1=Wani clan}}, whose ancestor is {{ill|Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|de=Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|german=Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|ja=天足彦国押人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443593}}, a {{ill|Prince|en|Prince|WD=Q2747456|lt=Prince|1=Prince}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. There is also an Ono clan derived from place names, such as the one known as the Ono clan of the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}}, claiming descent from {{ill|Minamoto no Moroyoshi|de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|german=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|ja=源盛義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564234}}, who resided in Mino Province Ono. == 動向 == It was based around Ono Village in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District|1=Shiga District, Shiga}}, in what is now part of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Otsu City|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is also believed that Ono Township in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}{{ill|Atago District|de=Atago-Kreis|german=Atago-Kreis|ja=愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, in what is now within {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, was under their control. Moreover, within the {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} area of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, at the {{ill|Sūdō Shrine|de=Sūdō-Schrein|german=Sūdō-Schrein|ja=崇導神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 崇道神社|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}}, on Mt. Jōkō in the Nishimyōji area, is the tomb of {{ill|Ono no Kejin|de=Ono no Kejin|german=Ono no Kejin|ja=小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Kejin|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}}. The Ono clan was in charge of many maritime and military affairs, drawing on their skills in water transportation on Lake Biwa{{ill|Inoue Mitsurō|de=Inoue Mitsurō|german=Inoue Mitsurō|ja=井上満郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsurō|1=Mitsuo Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16264281}}’s “{{ill|Ono no Imoko: The Envoy to Sui I (Part 1)|de=Ono no Imoko: Botschafter an die Sui (Teil 1)|german=Ono no Imoko: Der Gesandte der Sui (Teil 1)|ja=遣隋使の小野妹子①|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Imoko: The Envoy to Sui I (Part 1)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}” (column "People Who Come Across" in {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|en|Kyoto Shimbun|WD=Q6452210|lt=Kyoto Shimbun|1=Kyoto Shimbun}}, October 17, 2021). Many members of the clan served as envoys, including {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}}, who became an envoy to the Sui, as well as envoys to the Tang. Numerous local bureaucrats in regions such as {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku|1=Tōhoku region}} and {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}} also hailed from this clan. While renowned for excellence in Chinese poetry and waka, with figures like {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}} who rose to the position of counselor, and {{ill|Ono no Michikaze|en|Ono no Michikaze|WD=Q1439775|lt=Ono no Michikaze|1=Ono no Michikaze}}, known as a calligrapher, the clan also produced figures such as {{ill|Ono no Harukaze|de=Ono Harukaze|german=Ono Harukaze|ja=小野春風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Harukaze|1=Harukaze Ono|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464105}}, who became a {{ill|Chinju Shōgun|de=Chinju Shōgun|german=Chinju Shōgun|ja=鎮守将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju Shōgun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守府将軍|1=Chinjufu shōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2700052}}, and {{ill|Ono no Yoshikoku|de=Ono no Yoshikoku|german=Ono no Yoshikoku|ja=小野好古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Yoshikoku|1=Ono no Yoshifuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463979}}, who, as an officer tasked with pursuing criminals, helped suppress the rebellion known as {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Fujiwara no Jun'yū Rebellion|1=Tengyō no Ran}}. The leading faction of the {{ill|Musashi Seven Factions|de=Musashi Sieben Parteien|german=Musashi Sieben Parteien|ja=武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Seven Factions|1=Q11546323|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}, the {{ill|Yokoyama Faction|de=Yokoyama-Fraktion|german=Yokoyama-Fraktion|ja=横山党|lt=Yokoyama Clan|lt_ja=横山氏|lt_de=Yokoyama-Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Faction|1=Yokoyama Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542517}}, is descended from {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}}. A branch of the Yokoyama (Inomata) clan, the {{ill|Yurayoshi|de=Yurayoshi|german=Yurayoshi|ja=由良氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yurayoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横瀬氏|1=Q11543327|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543327}} (also known as {{ill|Yokose|de=Yokose|german=Yokose|ja=横瀬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokose|1=Q11543327|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543327}}), also bears Ono as its clan name. == 清和源氏義光流平賀氏系小野氏 == The branch of the Ono clan of the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}} traces its ancestry to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}’s fourth son, {{ill|Minamoto no Moroyoshi|de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|german=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|ja=源盛義|lt=Minamoto no Moroyoshi (Hiraga Moroyoshi)|lt_ja=源盛義（平賀盛義）|lt_de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi (Hiraga Moroyoshi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564234}}, and is known as the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}}. During the era of {{ill|Tameyoshi|de=Tameyoshi|german=Tameyoshi|ja=大内惟義|lt=Tameyoshi|lt_ja=惟義|lt_de=Tameyoshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tameyoshi|1=Ōuchi Koreyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432524}}, the clan obtained the governorships of six provinces in the Kinki region—Ise, Iga, Echizen, Mino, Tanba, and Settsu—and held an important position within the Kamakura shogunate. It is said that Tameyoshi’s grandson, Yoshiyuki, resided in Ono of Mino Province and took the name Ono. One member of this clan, Ono Tsuguatsu, served under {{ill|Oe no Chikahiro|de=Oe no Chikahiro|german=Oe no Chikahiro|ja=大江親広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe no Chikahiro|1=Ōe no Chikahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436889}}, the legitimate son of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Oe no Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, but when Chikahiro, having been defeated in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}, went into hiding at {{ill|Sagae Manor|de=Sagae-Anwesen|german=Sagae-Anwesen|ja=寒河江荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae Manor|1=Q17224643|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17224643}} in {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|WD=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province|1=Dewa Province}}, he accompanied him. In the Jōkyū War, the head of the Hiraga clan, who inherited governorships over six provinces, namely {{ill|Tamenobu|de=Tamenobu|german=Tamenobu|ja=大内惟信|lt=Tamenobu|lt_ja=惟信|lt_de=Tamenobu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamenobu|1=Ōuchi Korenobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432522}}, also served under {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}, and was later exiled. Ono Tsuguatsu’s son, Akeno, is believed to have resided in the Kantō region, as Chikahiro’s son, Hirosaki, who was permitted after the rebellion, served the shogunate in Kamakura. However, around the late 13th century, when Oe no Motoken entered Sagae Manor, the Ono clan is thought to have also migrated south. Subsequently, they served as retainers of the Sagae clan, but after the Sagae clan was destroyed by the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|WD=Q524625|lt=Mogami Clan|1=Mogami clan}}, in the 12th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1584), they returned to farming. == 系図 == {{ill|Oblique type|en|Oblique type|WD=Q3232321|lt=Italicized|1=Oblique type}} indicates female. ({{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}'s first {{ill|Prince|en|Prince|WD=Q2747456|lt=Prince|1=Prince}}) |Type = {{ill|Imperial Branch|de=Kaiserliche Linie|german=Kaiserliche Linie|ja=皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Branch|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} |Seat = {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} Ono Village

{{ill|Ono no Komachi|en|Ono no Komachi|WD=Q198984|lt=Ono no Komachi|1=Ono no Komachi}}
{{ill|Ono no Michikaze|en|Ono no Michikaze|WD=Q1439775|lt=Ono no Michikaze|1=Ono no Michikaze}}
{{ill|Ono no Kejin|de=Ono no Kejin|german=Ono no Kejin|ja=小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Kejin|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}}
{{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}} |Descendants = {{ill|Tokuoka Family|de=Tokuoka-Familie|german=Tokuoka-Familie|ja=徳岡家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuoka Family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Chika Family|de=Chika-Familie|german=Chika-Familie|ja=地下家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chika Family|1=Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}})
{{ill|Nagami Clan|de=Nagami-Clan|german=Nagami-Clan|ja=永見氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagami Clan|1=Q48762071|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48762071}} ({{ill|Warrior Family|de=Kriegerfamilie|german=Kriegerfamilie|ja=武家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Warrior Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buke|WD=Q2927809}})
{{ill|Yokoyama Faction|de=Yokoyama-Fraktion|german=Yokoyama-Fraktion|ja=横山党|lt=Yokoyama Clan|lt_ja=横山氏|lt_de=Yokoyama-Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Faction|1=Yokoyama Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542517}}
Others }} '''Ono-shi''' (小野氏) is a clan that takes "Ono" as its name, belonging to the {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Clan|1=Uji (clan)}} of {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clans|1=Clan}}. It was a clan active from the first half of the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th Century|1=7th century}} to the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}}. The {{ill|Surname|en|Surname|WD=Q101352|lt=Surname|1=Surname}} originally was that of a {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Retainer|1=Omi (title)}}, but was later elevated to {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Asomi|1=Ason}} by the {{ill|Eight-Colored Surname|de=Achtfarbeniger Familienname|german=Achtfarbeniger Familienname|ja=八色の姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Colored Surname|1=Eight Kabane system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391729}}. It is a branch of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Ware Clan|1=Wani clan}}, whose ancestor is {{ill|Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|de=Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|german=Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|ja=天足彦国押人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametarashi-hiko Kunioshi no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443593}}, a {{ill|Prince|en|Prince|WD=Q2747456|lt=Prince|1=Prince}} of {{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. There is also an Ono clan derived from place names, such as the one known as the Ono clan of the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}}, claiming descent from {{ill|Minamoto no Moroyoshi|de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|german=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|ja=源盛義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564234}}, who resided in Mino Province Ono. == 動向 == It was based around Ono Village in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}{{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District|1=Shiga District, Shiga}}, in what is now part of {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|WD=Q202907|lt=Otsu City|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is also believed that Ono Township in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}{{ill|Atago District|de=Atago-Kreis|german=Atago-Kreis|ja=愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, in what is now within {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, was under their control. Moreover, within the {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} area of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, at the {{ill|Sūdō Shrine|de=Sūdō-Schrein|german=Sūdō-Schrein|ja=崇導神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sūdō Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 崇道神社|1=Sudō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477104}}, on Mt. Jōkō in the Nishimyōji area, is the tomb of {{ill|Ono no Kejin|de=Ono no Kejin|german=Ono no Kejin|ja=小野毛人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Kejin|1=Ono no Emishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11034987}}. The Ono clan was in charge of many maritime and military affairs, drawing on their skills in water transportation on Lake Biwa{{ill|Inoue Mitsurō|de=Inoue Mitsurō|german=Inoue Mitsurō|ja=井上満郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsurō|1=Mitsuo Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16264281}}’s “{{ill|Ono no Imoko: The Envoy to Sui I (Part 1)|de=Ono no Imoko: Botschafter an die Sui (Teil 1)|german=Ono no Imoko: Der Gesandte der Sui (Teil 1)|ja=遣隋使の小野妹子①|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Imoko: The Envoy to Sui I (Part 1)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}” (column "People Who Come Across" in {{ill|Kyoto Shimbun|en|Kyoto Shimbun|WD=Q6452210|lt=Kyoto Shimbun|1=Kyoto Shimbun}}, October 17, 2021). Many members of the clan served as envoys, including {{ill|Ono no Imoko|en|Ono no Imoko|WD=Q1284072|lt=Ono no Imoko|1=Ono no Imoko}}, who became an envoy to the Sui, as well as envoys to the Tang. Numerous local bureaucrats in regions such as {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku|1=Tōhoku region}} and {{ill|Kyushu|en|Kyushu|WD=Q13987|lt=Kyushu|1=Kyushu}} also hailed from this clan. While renowned for excellence in Chinese poetry and waka, with figures like {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}} who rose to the position of counselor, and {{ill|Ono no Michikaze|en|Ono no Michikaze|WD=Q1439775|lt=Ono no Michikaze|1=Ono no Michikaze}}, known as a calligrapher, the clan also produced figures such as {{ill|Ono no Harukaze|de=Ono Harukaze|german=Ono Harukaze|ja=小野春風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Harukaze|1=Harukaze Ono|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464105}}, who became a {{ill|Chinju Shōgun|de=Chinju Shōgun|german=Chinju Shōgun|ja=鎮守将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinju Shōgun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守府将軍|1=Chinjufu shōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2700052}}, and {{ill|Ono no Yoshikoku|de=Ono no Yoshikoku|german=Ono no Yoshikoku|ja=小野好古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono no Yoshikoku|1=Ono no Yoshifuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463979}}, who, as an officer tasked with pursuing criminals, helped suppress the rebellion known as {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|WD=Q2975322|lt=Fujiwara no Jun'yū Rebellion|1=Tengyō no Ran}}. The leading faction of the {{ill|Musashi Seven Factions|de=Musashi Sieben Parteien|german=Musashi Sieben Parteien|ja=武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Seven Factions|1=Q11546323|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}, the {{ill|Yokoyama Faction|de=Yokoyama-Fraktion|german=Yokoyama-Fraktion|ja=横山党|lt=Yokoyama Clan|lt_ja=横山氏|lt_de=Yokoyama-Clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Faction|1=Yokoyama Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11542517}}, is descended from {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}}. A branch of the Yokoyama (Inomata) clan, the {{ill|Yurayoshi|de=Yurayoshi|german=Yurayoshi|ja=由良氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yurayoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横瀬氏|1=Q11543327|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543327}} (also known as {{ill|Yokose|de=Yokose|german=Yokose|ja=横瀬氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokose|1=Q11543327|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543327}}), also bears Ono as its clan name. == 清和源氏義光流平賀氏系小野氏 == The branch of the Ono clan of the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}} traces its ancestry to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}’s fourth son, {{ill|Minamoto no Moroyoshi|de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|german=Minamoto no Moroyoshi|ja=源盛義|lt=Minamoto no Moroyoshi (Hiraga Moroyoshi)|lt_ja=源盛義（平賀盛義）|lt_de=Minamoto no Moroyoshi (Hiraga Moroyoshi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moroyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564234}}, and is known as the {{ill|Hiraga Genji|de=Hiraga Genji|german=Hiraga Genji|ja=平賀氏|lt=Hiraga Genji|lt_ja=平賀源氏|lt_de=Hiraga Genji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraga Genji|1=Hiraga clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483568}}. During the era of {{ill|Tameyoshi|de=Tameyoshi|german=Tameyoshi|ja=大内惟義|lt=Tameyoshi|lt_ja=惟義|lt_de=Tameyoshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tameyoshi|1=Ōuchi Koreyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432524}}, the clan obtained the governorships of six provinces in the Kinki region—Ise, Iga, Echizen, Mino, Tanba, and Settsu—and held an important position within the Kamakura shogunate. It is said that Tameyoshi’s grandson, Yoshiyuki, resided in Ono of Mino Province and took the name Ono. One member of this clan, Ono Tsuguatsu, served under {{ill|Oe no Chikahiro|de=Oe no Chikahiro|german=Oe no Chikahiro|ja=大江親広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oe no Chikahiro|1=Ōe no Chikahiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436889}}, the legitimate son of {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Oe no Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}}, but when Chikahiro, having been defeated in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū War|1=Jōkyū War}}, went into hiding at {{ill|Sagae Manor|de=Sagae-Anwesen|german=Sagae-Anwesen|ja=寒河江荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagae Manor|1=Q17224643|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17224643}} in {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|WD=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province|1=Dewa Province}}, he accompanied him. In the Jōkyū War, the head of the Hiraga clan, who inherited governorships over six provinces, namely {{ill|Tamenobu|de=Tamenobu|german=Tamenobu|ja=大内惟信|lt=Tamenobu|lt_ja=惟信|lt_de=Tamenobu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamenobu|1=Ōuchi Korenobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432522}}, also served under {{ill|Emperor Go-Toba|en|Emperor Go-Toba|WD=Q316658|lt=Emperor Go-Toba|1=Emperor Go-Toba}}, and was later exiled. Ono Tsuguatsu’s son, Akeno, is believed to have resided in the Kantō region, as Chikahiro’s son, Hirosaki, who was permitted after the rebellion, served the shogunate in Kamakura. However, around the late 13th century, when Oe no Motoken entered Sagae Manor, the Ono clan is thought to have also migrated south. Subsequently, they served as retainers of the Sagae clan, but after the Sagae clan was destroyed by the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|WD=Q524625|lt=Mogami Clan|1=Mogami clan}}, in the 12th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1584), they returned to farming. == 系図 == {{ill|Oblique type|en|Oblique type|WD=Q3232321|lt=Italicized|1=Oblique type}} indicates female. '''{{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|WD=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}''' ┣━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓ {{ill|Ame no Oshitai Hiko no Mikoto|de=Ame no Oshitai Hiko no Mikoto|german=Ame no Oshitai Hiko no Mikoto|ja=天押帯日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Oshitai Hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天足彦国押人命|1=Prince Ametarashihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443593}} ┃ ┣━━━━━━━┓ ┃ {{ill|Wanari Hiko no Oshihito no Mikoto|de=Wanari Hiko no Oshihito no Mikoto|german=Wanari Hiko no Oshihito no Mikoto|ja=和邇日子押人彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanari Hiko no Oshihito no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''{{ill|Oshihime|de=Oshihime|german=Oshihime|ja=押媛|lt=Oshihime (Mikoto)|lt_ja=押媛命|lt_de=Oshihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihime|qq=}}''━━━━┳━━━━'''{{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan|1=Emperor Kōan}}''' ┣━━━━━━┓ ┃ {{ill|Hiko Umatsu no Mikoto|de=Hiko Umatsu no Mikoto|german=Hiko Umatsu no Mikoto|ja=彦姥津命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Umatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''{{ill|Hiko Ketobime no Mikoto|de=Hiko Ketobime no Mikoto|german=Hiko Ketobime no Mikoto|ja=彦祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Ketobime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' '''{{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}''' ┣━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━┓ {{ill|Itomitsu no Mikoto|de=Itomitsu no Mikoto|german=Itomitsu no Mikoto|ja=伊富都久命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itomitsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ''{{ill|Emon Ketobime no Mikoto|de=Emon Ketobime no Mikoto|german=Emon Ketobime no Mikoto|ja=袁祁都依比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon Ketobime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' {{ill|Hiko Kunifuki no Mikoto|de=Hiko Kunifuki no Mikoto|german=Hiko Kunifuki no Mikoto|ja=彦国葺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Kunifuki no Mikoto|1=Hikokuni-fuki no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487899}} ┏━━━━━━┫ {{ill|Tateyasune no Mikoto|de=Tateyasune no Mikoto|german=Tateyasune no Mikoto|ja=建耶須禰命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyasune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Okunō no Mikoto|de=Okunō no Mikoto|german=Okunō no Mikoto|ja=大口納命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okunō no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ┏━━━━━╋━━━━━━┓ {{ill|Marorokou no Mikoto|de=Marorokou no Mikoto|german=Marorokou no Mikoto|ja=真侶古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marorokou no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Hiko Naru no Mikoto|de=Hiko Naru no Mikoto|german=Hiko Naru no
